title: Objective-C++ Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Objective-C++ source files contain code that blends Objective-C and C++ in a single translation unit. This combination is common in Apple platform projects that need to interface high-level Objective-C frameworks with C++ libraries or game engines.

## When to use
Use [Objective-C++ Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:objcpp;textable;code) for `.mm` files. These files can freely intermix Objective-C classes, C++ classes, and plain C, making them the primary bridging mechanism between Apple's Cocoa/UIKit APIs and C++ codebases such as rendering engines or audio DSP libraries.

## Notes
Objective-C++ imposes restrictions on how Objective-C and C++ interact: Objective-C objects cannot inherit from C++ classes and vice versa, and C++ exceptions and Objective-C exceptions have distinct propagation rules. Compilers treat `.mm` files as Objective-C++ automatically; the file extension is the primary indicator of this mixed mode.
